title: Tailwind CSS Utilities Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of single-purpose helper classes for layout, spacing, colors, and responsive design tweaks.

questions.question: Which and/or what are 'utility classes' in Tailwind?
options: Small, single-purpose helper classes used to build custom UIs directly in HTML.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Which prefix would you use to apply a style only when the user hovers over an element in Tailwind?
What is the primary benefit of Tailwind's utility-first approach?
options: It allows for quick and consistent design adjustments without writing custom CSS.
It only works for mobile apps.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the correct naming convention for a margin-top of size 4 in Tailwind?
Do Tailwind utilities support responsive design using breakpoints like md or sm?
options: Yes, as they provide prefix classes (e.g., md:grid-cols-2 or sm:p-5) for different screens.
No, they only work for fixed widths.
